skill.name: notion-api
skill.description: This skill provides comprehensive instructions for interacting with the Notion API via REST calls. This skill should be used whenever the user asks to interact with Notion, including reading, creating, updating, or deleting pages, databases, blocks, comments, or any other Notion content. The skill covers authentication, all available endpoints, pagination, error handling, and best practices.
skill.summary: Enable Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to read, create, and manage Notion pages, databases, and blocks through the official REST API.
skill.author: intellectronica

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate Notion API documentation skill that requires network access to interact with the Notion API. Static analysis flagged 802 potential issues, but all are false positives: backtick detection triggered by markdown code fences, URL detection from official API documentation, and environment variable patterns from authentication examples. The skill implements proper security practices including user confirmation for destructive operations and explicit instructions to never expose API tokens.

low_findings.title: Network Access Required
low_findings.description: This skill makes REST API calls to api.notion.com. Network access is required for the skill to function.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network access is explicitly documented in SKILL.md with curl examples to api.notion.com endpoints. This is intentional and necessary for API functionality.

low_findings.title: API Key Environment Variable Access
low_findings.description: The skill accesses NOTION_API_TOKEN environment variable for authentication with the Notion API.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Environment variable access is documented as the authentication mechanism. This is standard API key handling practice, not a security vulnerability.

content.user_title: Connect to Notion workspaces via the official REST API
content.value_statement: Managing Notion content manually is time-consuming. This skill enables Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to programmatically read, create, and update pages, databases, and blocks through the official Notion API with proper authentication and error handling.
actual_capabilities: Authenticate with Notion using Bearer tokens stored in environment variables
Search across all accessible Notion pages and databases
Create, read, update, and delete pages and databases
Query databases with filters, sorts, and pagination
Work with all Notion block types including paragraphs, headings, lists, tables, and embeds
Handle rich text formatting with annotations like bold, italic, code, and links
limitations: Does not support Notion desktop app features like inline databases or synced blocks
Cannot manage workspace settings, users, or permissions
Maximum 3 requests per second per integration (rate limiting applies)
Cannot process files or media directly; must use Notion-hosted URLs

use_cases.title: Build knowledge bases from Notion
use_cases.description: Automatically sync project documentation, meeting notes, or research materials from Notion databases into other systems or formats.
use_cases.target_user: Developers building integrations with Notion-powered workflows

use_cases.title: Generate reports from Notion data
use_cases.description: Query Notion databases with specific filters and aggregate data into summaries, dashboards, or automated status reports.
use_cases.target_user: Project managers tracking progress across Notion workspaces

use_cases.title: Automate Notion content creation
use_cases.description: Create structured pages, update properties, or add blocks to existing pages based on external triggers or scheduled workflows.
use_cases.target_user: Automation engineers setting up Notion-based pipelines

prompt_templates.title: Search Notion for content
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Notion for pages containing "{query}" and show me the titles and IDs of the results.
prompt_templates.scenario: When users want to find existing Notion pages or databases

prompt_templates.title: Create a new page in a database
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new page in the {database_name} database with these properties: {property_values}. Use the Notion API to add this page.
prompt_templates.scenario: When users want to add entries to a Notion database

prompt_templates.title: Query database with filters
prompt_templates.prompt: Query the {database_name} database and return all items where {filter_condition}. Format the results as a markdown table.
prompt_templates.scenario: When users want filtered views of Notion database content

prompt_templates.title: Update existing content
prompt_templates.prompt: Update the Notion page with ID {page_id} by adding a new section with the heading "{heading}" and content "{content}".
prompt_templates.scenario: When users want to modify or extend existing Notion pages

best_practices: Always verify authentication by testing with the bot user endpoint before making other API calls
Implement exponential backoff when receiving 429 rate limit responses rather than retrying immediately
Request user confirmation before any destructive operations like deleting or archiving pages
anti_patterns: Do not expose API tokens in logs, error messages, or responses - only use them in Authorization headers
Avoid making requests without handling rate limiting - this will result in failed operations
Do not skip user confirmation for bulk operations or schema changes to databases

faq.question: How do I get started with this skill?
faq.answer: Set the NOTION_API_TOKEN environment variable with your Notion integration token. The skill will automatically use this for authentication. If missing, it will prompt you for the token.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with any Notion workspace?
faq.answer: No. The Notion integration must be connected to the workspace and have appropriate permissions for the pages and databases you want to access. Share the specific pages with your integration in Notion.

faq.question: What happens if I exceed the rate limit?
faq.answer: The Notion API returns HTTP 429 when rate limited. The skill recommends implementing exponential backoff and respecting the Retry-After header in responses.

faq.question: Can I create nested pages or blocks?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports creating child blocks and nested page structures. However, some block types cannot have children, so check the block type documentation for limitations.

faq.question: How do I handle authentication errors?
faq.answer: Verify your NOTION_API_TOKEN is valid and has not expired. Check that the integration has been shared with the specific workspace and pages you are trying to access.

faq.question: What Notion API version does this skill use?
faq.answer: This skill uses Notion API version 2025-09-03, which is specified in all API request headers. This ensures compatibility with the latest Notion API features.
